moduleDescription:
The tables module implements variants of an efficient hash table (also often named dictionary in other programming languages) that is a mapping from keys to values.

There are several different types of hash tables available:

  • Table is the usual hash table,
  • OrderedTable is like Table but remembers insertion order,
  • CountTable is a mapping from a key to its number of occurrences

For consistency with every other data type in Nim these have value semantics, this means that = performs a copy of the hash table.

For ref semantics use their Ref variants: TableRef, OrderedTableRef, and CountTableRef.

To give an example, when a is a Table, then var b = a gives b as a new independent table. b is initialised with the contents of a. Changing b does not affect a and vice versa:

On the other hand, when a is a TableRef instead, then changes to b also affect a. Both a and b ref the same data structure:

Basic usage

Table

OrderedTable

OrderedTable is used when it is important to preserve the insertion order of keys.

CountTable

CountTable is useful for counting number of items of some container (e.g. string, sequence or array), as it is a mapping where the items are the keys, and their number of occurrences are the values. For that purpose toCountTable proc comes in handy:

The same could have been achieved by manually iterating over a container and increasing each key's value with inc proc:

Hashing

If you are using simple standard types like int or string for the keys of the table you won't have any problems, but as soon as you try to use a more complex object as a key you will be greeted by a strange compiler error:

Error: type mismatch: got (Person)
but expected one of:
hashes.hash(x: openArray[A]): Hash
hashes.hash(x: int): Hash
hashes.hash(x: float): Hash

What is happening here is that the types used for table keys require to have a hash() proc which will convert them to a Hash value, and the compiler is listing all the hash functions it knows. Additionally there has to be a == operator that provides the same semantics as its corresponding hash proc.

After you add hash and == for your custom type everything will work. Currently, however, hash for objects is not defined, whereas system.== for objects does exist and performs a "deep" comparison (every field is compared) which is usually what you want. So in the following example implementing only hash suffices:

See also

  • json module for table-like structure which allows heterogeneous members
  • strtabs module for efficient hash tables mapping from strings to strings
  • hashes module for helper functions for hashing

entries.name: Table
entries.code: Table[A; B] = object
entries.description:
Generic hash table, consisting of a key-value pair.

data and counter are internal implementation details which can't be accessed.

For creating an empty Table, use initTable proc.

entries.name: TableRef
entries.code: TableRef[A; B] = ref Table[A, B]
entries.description:
Ref version of Table.

For creating a new empty TableRef, use newTable proc.

entries.name: defaultInitialSize
entries.code: defaultInitialSize = 32

entries.name: initTable
entries.code: proc initTable[A, B](initialSize = defaultInitialSize): Table[A, B]
entries.description:
Creates a new hash table that is empty.

Starting from Nim v0.20, tables are initialized by default and it is not necessary to call this function explicitly.

See also:

  • toTable proc
  • newTable proc for creating a TableRef

entries.code: proc `[]=`[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A; val: sink B)
entries.description:
Inserts a (key, value) pair into t.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • del proc for removing a key from the table

entries.name: toTable
entries.code: proc toTable[A, B](pairs: openArray[(A, B)]): Table[A, B]
entries.description:
Creates a new hash table that contains the given pairs.

pairs is a container consisting of (key, value) tuples.

See also:

  • initTable proc
  • newTable proc for a TableRef version

entries.code: proc `[]`[A, B](t: Table[A, B]; key: A): lent B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key].

If key is not in t, the KeyError exception is raised. One can check with hasKey proc whether the key exists.

See also:

  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist
  • []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.code: proc `[]`[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A): var B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key]. The value can be modified.

If key is not in t, the KeyError exception is raised.

See also:

  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist
  • []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.name: hasKey
entries.code: proc hasKey[A, B](t: Table[A, B]; key: A): bool
entries.description:
Returns true if key is in the table t.

See also:

  • contains proc for use with the in operator
  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: contains
entries.code: proc contains[A, B](t: Table[A, B]; key: A): bool
entries.description: Alias of hasKey proc for use with the in operator.

entries.name: hasKeyOrPut
entries.code: proc hasKeyOrPut[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A; val: B): bool
entries.description:
Returns true if key is in the table, otherwise inserts value.

See also:

  • hasKey proc
  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.code: proc getOrDefault[A, B](t: Table[A, B]; key: A): B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise, the default initialization value for type B is returned (e.g. 0 for any integer type).

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.code: proc getOrDefault[A, B](t: Table[A, B]; key: A; def: B): B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise, def is returned.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: mgetOrPut
entries.code: proc mgetOrPut[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A; val: B): var B
entries.description:
Retrieves value at t[key] or puts val if not present, either way returning a value which can be modified.

Note that while the value returned is of type var B, it is easy to accidentally create a copy of the value at t[key]. Remember that seqs and strings are value types, and therefore cannot be copied into a separate variable for modification. See the example below.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: mgetOrPut
entries.code: proc mgetOrPut[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A): var B
entries.description: Retrieves the value at t[key] or puts the default initialization value for type B (e.g. 0 for any integer type).

entries.name: len
entries.code: proc len[A, B](t: Table[A, B]): int
entries.description: Returns the number of keys in t.

entries.name: add
entries.code: proc add[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A; val: sink B) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; it was more confusing than useful, use `[]=`".}

pragmas: deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; it was more confusing than useful, use `[]=`"

entries.description:
Puts a new (key, value) pair into t even if t[key] already exists.

This can introduce duplicate keys into the table!

Use []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table without introducing duplicates.

entries.name: del
entries.code: proc del[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A)
entries.description:
Deletes key from hash table t. Does nothing if the key does not exist.
Warning: If duplicate keys were added (via the now deprecated add proc), this may need to be called multiple times.

See also:

  • pop proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: pop
entries.code: proc pop[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A; val: var B): bool
entries.description:
Deletes the key from the table. Returns true, if the key existed, and sets val to the mapping of the key. Otherwise, returns false, and the val is unchanged.
Warning: If duplicate keys were added (via the now deprecated add proc), this may need to be called multiple times.

See also:

  • del proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: take
entries.code: proc take[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A; val: var B): bool {.inline.}
entries.description:
Alias for:
  • pop proc

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear[A, B](t: var Table[A, B])
entries.description:
Resets the table so that it is empty.

See also:

  • del proc
  • pop proc

entries.code: proc `$`[A, B](t: Table[A, B]): string
entries.description: The $ operator for hash tables. Used internally when calling echo on a table.

entries.code: proc `==`[A, B](s, t: Table[A, B]): bool
entries.description: The == operator for hash tables. Returns true if the content of both tables contains the same key-value pairs. Insert order does not matter.

entries.name: indexBy
entries.code: proc indexBy[A, B, C](collection: A; index: proc (x: B): C): Table[C, B]
entries.description: Index the collection with the proc provided.

entries.name: withValue
entries.code: template withValue[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A; value, body: untyped)
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key].

value can be modified in the scope of the withValue call.

entries.name: withValue
entries.code:
template withValue[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]; key: A;
                         value, body1, body2: untyped)
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key].

value can be modified in the scope of the withValue call.

entries.name: withValue
entries.code: template withValue[A, B](t: Table[A, B]; key: A; value, body1, body2: untyped)
entries.description: Retrieves the value at t[key] if it exists, assigns it to the variable value and executes body

entries.name: withValue
entries.code: template withValue[A, B](t: Table[A, B]; key: A; value, body: untyped)
entries.description: Retrieves the value at t[key] if it exists, assigns it to the variable value and executes body

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[A, B](t: Table[A, B]): (A, B)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • values iterator

Examples:

let a = {
  'o': [1, 5, 7, 9],
  'e': [2, 4, 6, 8]
  }.toTable

for k, v in a.pairs:
  echo "key: ", k
  echo "value: ", v

# key: e
# value: [2, 4, 6, 8]
# key: o
# value: [1, 5, 7, 9]

entries.name: mpairs
entries.code: iterator mpairs[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]): (A, var B)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t (must be declared as var). The values can be modified.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: keys
entries.code: iterator keys[A, B](t: Table[A, B]): lent A
entries.description:
Iterates over any key in the table t.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: values
entries.code: iterator values[A, B](t: Table[A, B]): lent B
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: mvalues
entries.code: iterator mvalues[A, B](t: var Table[A, B]): var B
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t (must be declared as var). The values can be modified.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: allValues
entries.code: iterator allValues[A, B](t: Table[A, B]; key: A): B {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; tables with duplicated keys are deprecated".}

pragmas: deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; tables with duplicated keys are deprecated"

entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t that belongs to the given key.

Used if you have a table with duplicate keys (as a result of using add proc).

entries.name: newTable
entries.code: proc newTable[A, B](initialSize = defaultInitialSize): TableRef[A, B]
entries.description:
Creates a new ref hash table that is empty.

See also:

  • newTable proc for creating a TableRef from a collection of (key, value) pairs
  • initTable proc for creating a Table

entries.name: newTable
entries.code: proc newTable[A, B](pairs: openArray[(A, B)]): TableRef[A, B]
entries.description:
Creates a new ref hash table that contains the given pairs.

pairs is a container consisting of (key, value) tuples.

See also:

  • newTable proc
  • toTable proc for a Table version

entries.name: newTableFrom
entries.code: proc newTableFrom[A, B, C](collection: A; index: proc (x: B): C): TableRef[C, B]
entries.description: Index the collection with the proc provided.

entries.code: proc `[]`[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A): var B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key].

If key is not in t, the KeyError exception is raised. One can check with hasKey proc whether the key exists.

See also:

  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist
  • []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.code: proc `[]=`[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: sink B)
entries.description:
Inserts a (key, value) pair into t.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • del proc for removing a key from the table

entries.name: hasKey
entries.code: proc hasKey[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A): bool
entries.description:
Returns true if key is in the table t.

See also:

  • contains proc for use with the in operator
  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: contains
entries.code: proc contains[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A): bool
entries.description: Alias of hasKey proc for use with the in operator.

entries.name: hasKeyOrPut
entries.code: proc hasKeyOrPut[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: B): bool
entries.description:
Returns true if key is in the table, otherwise inserts value.

See also:

  • hasKey proc
  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.code: proc getOrDefault[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A): B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise, the default initialization value for type B is returned (e.g. 0 for any integer type).

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.code: proc getOrDefault[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A; def: B): B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise, def is returned.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: mgetOrPut
entries.code: proc mgetOrPut[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: B): var B
entries.description:
Retrieves value at t[key] or puts val if not present, either way returning a value which can be modified.

Note that while the value returned is of type var B, it is easy to accidentally create an copy of the value at t[key]. Remember that seqs and strings are value types, and therefore cannot be copied into a separate variable for modification. See the example below.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: mgetOrPut
entries.code: proc mgetOrPut[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A): var B
entries.description: Retrieves the value at t[key] or puts the default initialization value for type B (e.g. 0 for any integer type).

entries.name: len
entries.code: proc len[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]): int
entries.description: Returns the number of keys in t.

entries.name: add
entries.code: proc add[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: sink B) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; it was more confusing than useful, use `[]=`".}

pragmas: deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; it was more confusing than useful, use `[]=`"

entries.description:
Puts a new (key, value) pair into t even if t[key] already exists.

This can introduce duplicate keys into the table!

Use []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table without introducing duplicates.

entries.name: del
entries.code: proc del[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A)
entries.description:
Deletes key from hash table t. Does nothing if the key does not exist.
Warning: If duplicate keys were added (via the now deprecated add proc), this may need to be called multiple times.

See also:

  • pop proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: pop
entries.code: proc pop[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: var B): bool
entries.description:
Deletes the key from the table. Returns true, if the key existed, and sets val to the mapping of the key. Otherwise, returns false, and the val is unchanged.
Warning: If duplicate keys were added (via the now deprecated add proc), this may need to be called multiple times.

See also:

  • del proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: take
entries.code: proc take[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: var B): bool {.inline.}
entries.description:
Alias for:
  • pop proc

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B])
entries.description:
Resets the table so that it is empty.

See also:

  • del proc
  • pop proc

entries.code: proc `$`[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]): string
entries.description: The $ operator for hash tables. Used internally when calling echo on a table.

entries.code: proc `==`[A, B](s, t: TableRef[A, B]): bool
entries.description: The == operator for hash tables. Returns true if either both tables are nil, or neither is nil and the content of both tables contains the same key-value pairs. Insert order does not matter.

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]): (A, B)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • values iterator

Examples:

let a = {
  'o': [1, 5, 7, 9],
  'e': [2, 4, 6, 8]
  }.newTable

for k, v in a.pairs:
  echo "key: ", k
  echo "value: ", v

# key: e
# value: [2, 4, 6, 8]
# key: o
# value: [1, 5, 7, 9]

entries.name: mpairs
entries.code: iterator mpairs[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]): (A, var B)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t. The values can be modified.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: keys
entries.code: iterator keys[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]): lent A
entries.description:
Iterates over any key in the table t.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: values
entries.code: iterator values[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]): lent B
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: mvalues
entries.code: iterator mvalues[A, B](t: TableRef[A, B]): var B
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t. The values can be modified.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: OrderedTable
entries.code: OrderedTable[A; B] = object
entries.description:
Hash table that remembers insertion order.

For creating an empty OrderedTable, use initOrderedTable proc.

entries.name: OrderedTableRef
entries.code: OrderedTableRef[A; B] = ref OrderedTable[A, B]
entries.description:
Ref version of OrderedTable.

For creating a new empty OrderedTableRef, use newOrderedTable proc.

entries.name: initOrderedTable
entries.code: proc initOrderedTable[A, B](initialSize = defaultInitialSize): OrderedTable[A, B]
entries.description:
Creates a new ordered hash table that is empty.

Starting from Nim v0.20, tables are initialized by default and it is not necessary to call this function explicitly.

See also:

  • toOrderedTable proc
  • newOrderedTable proc for creating an OrderedTableRef

entries.code: proc `[]=`[A, B](t: var OrderedTable[A, B]; key: A; val: sink B)
entries.description:
Inserts a (key, value) pair into t.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • del proc for removing a key from the table

entries.name: toOrderedTable
entries.code: proc toOrderedTable[A, B](pairs: openArray[(A, B)]): OrderedTable[A, B]
entries.description:
Creates a new ordered hash table that contains the given pairs.

pairs is a container consisting of (key, value) tuples.

See also:

  • initOrderedTable proc
  • newOrderedTable proc for an OrderedTableRef version

entries.code: proc `[]`[A, B](t: OrderedTable[A, B]; key: A): lent B

If key is not in t, the KeyError exception is raised. One can check with hasKey proc whether the key exists.

  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.name: mgetOrPut
entries.code: proc mgetOrPut[A, B](t: var OrderedTable[A, B]; key: A): var B
entries.description: Retrieves the value at t[key] or puts the default initialization value for type B (e.g. 0 for any integer type).

entries.name: len
entries.code: proc len[A, B](t: OrderedTable[A, B]): int {.inline.}
entries.description: Returns the number of keys in t.

entries.name: add
entries.code: proc add[A, B](t: var OrderedTable[A, B]; key: A; val: sink B) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; it was more confusing than useful, use `[]=`".}

pragmas: deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; it was more confusing than useful, use `[]=`"

entries.description:
Puts a new (key, value) pair into t even if t[key] already exists.

This can introduce duplicate keys into the table!

Use []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table without introducing duplicates.

entries.name: del
entries.code: proc del[A, B](t: var OrderedTable[A, B]; key: A)
entries.description:
Deletes key from hash table t. Does nothing if the key does not exist.

O(n) complexity.

See also:

  • pop proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: pop
entries.code: proc pop[A, B](t: var OrderedTable[A, B]; key: A; val: var B): bool
entries.description:
Deletes the key from the table. Returns true, if the key existed, and sets val to the mapping of the key. Otherwise, returns false, and the val is unchanged.

O(n) complexity.

See also:

  • del proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear[A, B](t: var OrderedTable[A, B])
entries.description:
Resets the table so that it is empty.

See also:

  • del proc
  • pop proc

entries.name: sort
entries.code:
proc sort[A, B](t: var OrderedTable[A, B]; cmp: proc (x, y: (A, B)): int;
                order = SortOrder.Ascending) {.effectsOf: cmp.}
entries.description:
Sorts t according to the function cmp.

This modifies the internal list that kept the insertion order, so insertion order is lost after this call but key lookup and insertions remain possible after sort (in contrast to the sort proc for count tables).

entries.code: proc `$`[A, B](t: OrderedTable[A, B]): string
entries.description: The $ operator for ordered hash tables. Used internally when calling echo on a table.

entries.code: proc `==`[A, B](s, t: OrderedTable[A, B]): bool
entries.description: The == operator for ordered hash tables. Returns true if both the content and the order are equal.

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[A, B](t: OrderedTable[A, B]): (A, B)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t in insertion order.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • values iterator

Examples:

let a = {
  'o': [1, 5, 7, 9],
  'e': [2, 4, 6, 8]
  }.toOrderedTable

for k, v in a.pairs:
  echo "key: ", k
  echo "value: ", v

# key: o
# value: [1, 5, 7, 9]
# key: e
# value: [2, 4, 6, 8]

entries.name: mpairs
entries.code: iterator mpairs[A, B](t: var OrderedTable[A, B]): (A, var B)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t (must be declared as var) in insertion order. The values can be modified.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: keys
entries.code: iterator keys[A, B](t: OrderedTable[A, B]): lent A
entries.description:
Iterates over any key in the table t in insertion order.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: values
entries.code: iterator values[A, B](t: OrderedTable[A, B]): lent B
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t in insertion order.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: mvalues
entries.code: iterator mvalues[A, B](t: var OrderedTable[A, B]): var B
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t (must be declared as var) in insertion order. The values can be modified.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: newOrderedTable
entries.code:
proc newOrderedTable[A, B](initialSize = defaultInitialSize): OrderedTableRef[A,
    B]
entries.description:
Creates a new ordered ref hash table that is empty.

See also:

  • newOrderedTable proc for creating an OrderedTableRef from a collection of (key, value) pairs
  • initOrderedTable proc for creating an OrderedTable

entries.name: newOrderedTable
entries.code: proc newOrderedTable[A, B](pairs: openArray[(A, B)]): OrderedTableRef[A, B]
entries.description:
Creates a new ordered ref hash table that contains the given pairs.

pairs is a container consisting of (key, value) tuples.

See also:

  • newOrderedTable proc
  • toOrderedTable proc for an OrderedTable version

entries.code: proc `[]`[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A): var B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key].

If key is not in t, the KeyError exception is raised. One can check with hasKey proc whether the key exists.

See also:

  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist
  • []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.code: proc `[]=`[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: sink B)
entries.description:
Inserts a (key, value) pair into t.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • del proc for removing a key from the table

entries.name: hasKey
entries.code: proc hasKey[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A): bool
entries.description:
Returns true if key is in the table t.

See also:

  • contains proc for use with the in operator
  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: contains
entries.code: proc contains[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A): bool
entries.description: Alias of hasKey proc for use with the in operator.

entries.name: hasKeyOrPut
entries.code: proc hasKeyOrPut[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: B): bool
entries.description:
Returns true if key is in the table, otherwise inserts value.

See also:

  • hasKey proc
  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.code: proc getOrDefault[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A): B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise, the default initialization value for type B is returned (e.g. 0 for any integer type).

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.code: proc getOrDefault[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A; def: B): B
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise, def is returned.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • mgetOrPut proc
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: mgetOrPut
entries.code: proc mgetOrPut[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: B): var B
entries.description:
Retrieves value at t[key] or puts val if not present, either way returning a value which can be modified.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc
  • hasKeyOrPut proc
  • getOrDefault proc to return a default value (e.g. zero for int) if the key doesn't exist
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: mgetOrPut
entries.code: proc mgetOrPut[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A): var B
entries.description: Retrieves the value at t[key] or puts the default initialization value for type B (e.g. 0 for any integer type).

entries.name: len
entries.code: proc len[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]): int {.inline.}
entries.description: Returns the number of keys in t.

entries.name: add
entries.code: proc add[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: sink B) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; it was more confusing than useful, use `[]=`".}

pragmas: deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.4; it was more confusing than useful, use `[]=`"

entries.description:
Puts a new (key, value) pair into t even if t[key] already exists.

This can introduce duplicate keys into the table!

Use []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table without introducing duplicates.

entries.name: del
entries.code: proc del[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A)
entries.description:
Deletes key from hash table t. Does nothing if the key does not exist.

See also:

  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: pop
entries.code: proc pop[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; key: A; val: var B): bool
entries.description:
Deletes the key from the table. Returns true, if the key existed, and sets val to the mapping of the key. Otherwise, returns false, and the val is unchanged.

See also:

  • del proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B])
entries.description:
Resets the table so that it is empty.

See also:

  • del proc

entries.name: sort
entries.code:
proc sort[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]; cmp: proc (x, y: (A, B)): int;
                order = SortOrder.Ascending) {.effectsOf: cmp.}
entries.description:
Sorts t according to the function cmp.

This modifies the internal list that kept the insertion order, so insertion order is lost after this call but key lookup and insertions remain possible after sort (in contrast to the sort proc for count tables).

entries.code: proc `$`[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]): string
entries.description: The $ operator for hash tables. Used internally when calling echo on a table.

entries.code: proc `==`[A, B](s, t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]): bool
entries.description: The == operator for ordered hash tables. Returns true if either both tables are nil, or neither is nil and the content and the order of both are equal.

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]): (A, B)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t in insertion order.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • values iterator

Examples:

let a = {
  'o': [1, 5, 7, 9],
  'e': [2, 4, 6, 8]
  }.newOrderedTable

for k, v in a.pairs:
  echo "key: ", k
  echo "value: ", v

# key: o
# value: [1, 5, 7, 9]
# key: e
# value: [2, 4, 6, 8]

entries.name: mpairs
entries.code: iterator mpairs[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]): (A, var B)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t in insertion order. The values can be modified.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: keys
entries.code: iterator keys[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]): lent A
entries.description:
Iterates over any key in the table t in insertion order.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: values
entries.code: iterator values[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]): lent B
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t in insertion order.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: mvalues
entries.code: iterator mvalues[A, B](t: OrderedTableRef[A, B]): var B
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t in insertion order. The values can be modified.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: CountTable
entries.code: CountTable[A] = object
entries.description:
Hash table that counts the number of each key. Unlike Table, this uses a zero count to signal "empty" & so does not cache hash values for comparison reduction or resize acceleration.

For creating an empty CountTable, use initCountTable proc.

entries.name: CountTableRef
entries.code: CountTableRef[A] = ref CountTable[A]
entries.description:
Ref version of CountTable.

For creating a new empty CountTableRef, use newCountTable proc.

entries.name: inc
entries.code: proc inc[A](t: var CountTable[A]; key: A; val = 1)

entries.name: initCountTable
entries.code: proc initCountTable[A](initialSize = defaultInitialSize): CountTable[A]
entries.description:
Creates a new count table that is empty.

Starting from Nim v0.20, tables are initialized by default and it is not necessary to call this function explicitly.

See also:

  • toCountTable proc
  • newCountTable proc for creating a CountTableRef

entries.name: toCountTable
entries.code: proc toCountTable[A](keys: openArray[A]): CountTable[A]
entries.description: Creates a new count table with every member of a container keys having a count of how many times it occurs in that container.

entries.code: proc `[]`[A](t: CountTable[A]; key: A): int
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise 0 is returned.

See also:

  • getOrDefault to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist
  • []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.code: proc `[]=`[A](t: var CountTable[A]; key: A; val: int)
entries.description:
Inserts a (key, value) pair into t.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • inc proc for incrementing a value of a key

entries.name: len
entries.code: proc len[A](t: CountTable[A]): int
entries.description: Returns the number of keys in t.

entries.name: smallest
entries.code: proc smallest[A](t: CountTable[A]): tuple[key: A, val: int]
entries.description:
Returns the (key, value) pair with the smallest val. Efficiency: O(n)

See also:

  • largest proc

entries.name: largest
entries.code: proc largest[A](t: CountTable[A]): tuple[key: A, val: int]
entries.description:
Returns the (key, value) pair with the largest val. Efficiency: O(n)

See also:

  • smallest proc

entries.name: hasKey
entries.code: proc hasKey[A](t: CountTable[A]; key: A): bool
entries.description:
Returns true if key is in the table t.

See also:

  • contains proc for use with the in operator
  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: contains
entries.code: proc contains[A](t: CountTable[A]; key: A): bool
entries.description: Alias of hasKey proc for use with the in operator.

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.code: proc getOrDefault[A](t: CountTable[A]; key: A; def: int = 0): int
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise, the integer value of def is returned.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.name: del
entries.code: proc del[A](t: var CountTable[A]; key: A)
entries.description:
Deletes key from table t. Does nothing if the key does not exist.

See also:

  • pop proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: pop
entries.code: proc pop[A](t: var CountTable[A]; key: A; val: var int): bool
entries.description:
Deletes the key from the table. Returns true, if the key existed, and sets val to the mapping of the key. Otherwise, returns false, and the val is unchanged.

See also:

  • del proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear[A](t: var CountTable[A])
entries.description:
Resets the table so that it is empty.

See also:

  • del proc
  • pop proc

entries.name: sort
entries.code: proc sort[A](t: var CountTable[A]; order = SortOrder.Descending)
entries.description:
Sorts the count table so that, by default, the entry with the highest counter comes first.
Warning: This is destructive! Once sorted, you must not modify t afterwards!

You can use the iterators pairs, keys, and values to iterate over t in the sorted order.

entries.name: merge
entries.code: proc merge[A](s: var CountTable[A]; t: CountTable[A])
entries.description: Merges the second table into the first one (must be declared as var).

entries.code: proc `$`[A](t: CountTable[A]): string
entries.description: The $ operator for count tables. Used internally when calling echo on a table.

entries.code: proc `==`[A](s, t: CountTable[A]): bool
entries.description: The == operator for count tables. Returns true if both tables contain the same keys with the same count. Insert order does not matter.

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[A](t: CountTable[A]): (A, int)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • values iterator

Examples:

let a = toCountTable("abracadabra")

for k, v in pairs(a):
  echo "key: ", k
  echo "value: ", v

# key: a
# value: 5
# key: b
# value: 2
# key: c
# value: 1
# key: d
# value: 1
# key: r
# value: 2

entries.name: mpairs
entries.code: iterator mpairs[A](t: var CountTable[A]): (A, var int)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t (must be declared as var). The values can be modified.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: keys
entries.code: iterator keys[A](t: CountTable[A]): lent A
entries.description:
Iterates over any key in the table t.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: values
entries.code: iterator values[A](t: CountTable[A]): int
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: mvalues
entries.code: iterator mvalues[A](t: var CountTable[A]): var int
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t (must be declared as var). The values can be modified.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: inc
entries.code: proc inc[A](t: CountTableRef[A]; key: A; val = 1)

entries.name: newCountTable
entries.code: proc newCountTable[A](initialSize = defaultInitialSize): CountTableRef[A]
entries.description:
Creates a new ref count table that is empty.

See also:

  • newCountTable proc for creating a CountTableRef from a collection
  • initCountTable proc for creating a CountTable

entries.name: newCountTable
entries.code: proc newCountTable[A](keys: openArray[A]): CountTableRef[A]
entries.description: Creates a new ref count table with every member of a container keys having a count of how many times it occurs in that container.

entries.code: proc `[]`[A](t: CountTableRef[A]; key: A): int
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise 0 is returned.

See also:

  • getOrDefault to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist
  • inc proc to inc even if missing
  • []= proc for inserting a new (key, value) pair in the table
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.code: proc `[]=`[A](t: CountTableRef[A]; key: A; val: int)
entries.description:
Inserts a (key, value) pair into t.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • inc proc for incrementing a value of a key

entries.name: smallest
entries.code: proc smallest[A](t: CountTableRef[A]): tuple[key: A, val: int]
entries.description:
Returns the (key, value) pair with the smallest val. Efficiency: O(n)

See also:

  • largest proc

entries.name: largest
entries.code: proc largest[A](t: CountTableRef[A]): tuple[key: A, val: int]
entries.description:
Returns the (key, value) pair with the largest val. Efficiency: O(n)

See also:

  • smallest proc

entries.name: hasKey
entries.code: proc hasKey[A](t: CountTableRef[A]; key: A): bool
entries.description:
Returns true if key is in the table t.

See also:

  • contains proc for use with the in operator
  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • getOrDefault proc to return a custom value if the key doesn't exist

entries.name: contains
entries.code: proc contains[A](t: CountTableRef[A]; key: A): bool
entries.description: Alias of hasKey proc for use with the in operator.

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.code: proc getOrDefault[A](t: CountTableRef[A]; key: A; def: int): int
entries.description:
Retrieves the value at t[key] if key is in t. Otherwise, the integer value of def is returned.

See also:

  • [] proc for retrieving a value of a key
  • hasKey proc for checking if a key is in the table

entries.name: len
entries.code: proc len[A](t: CountTableRef[A]): int
entries.description: Returns the number of keys in t.

entries.name: del
entries.code: proc del[A](t: CountTableRef[A]; key: A)
entries.description:
Deletes key from table t. Does nothing if the key does not exist.

See also:

  • pop proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: pop
entries.code: proc pop[A](t: CountTableRef[A]; key: A; val: var int): bool
entries.description:
Deletes the key from the table. Returns true, if the key existed, and sets val to the mapping of the key. Otherwise, returns false, and the val is unchanged.

See also:

  • del proc
  • clear proc to empty the whole table

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear[A](t: CountTableRef[A])
entries.description:
Resets the table so that it is empty.

See also:

  • del proc
  • pop proc

entries.name: sort
entries.code: proc sort[A](t: CountTableRef[A]; order = SortOrder.Descending)
entries.description:
Sorts the count table so that, by default, the entry with the highest counter comes first.

This is destructive! You must not modify `t` afterwards!

You can use the iterators pairs, keys, and values to iterate over t in the sorted order.

entries.name: merge
entries.code: proc merge[A](s, t: CountTableRef[A])
entries.description: Merges the second table into the first one.

entries.code: proc `$`[A](t: CountTableRef[A]): string
entries.description: The $ operator for count tables. Used internally when calling echo on a table.

entries.code: proc `==`[A](s, t: CountTableRef[A]): bool
entries.description: The == operator for count tables. Returns true if either both tables are nil, or neither is nil and both contain the same keys with the same count. Insert order does not matter.

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[A](t: CountTableRef[A]): (A, int)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • values iterator

Examples:

let a = newCountTable("abracadabra")

for k, v in pairs(a):
  echo "key: ", k
  echo "value: ", v

# key: a
# value: 5
# key: b
# value: 2
# key: c
# value: 1
# key: d
# value: 1
# key: r
# value: 2

entries.name: mpairs
entries.code: iterator mpairs[A](t: CountTableRef[A]): (A, var int)
entries.description:
Iterates over any (key, value) pair in the table t. The values can be modified.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: keys
entries.code: iterator keys[A](t: CountTableRef[A]): A
entries.description:
Iterates over any key in the table t.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: values
entries.code: iterator values[A](t: CountTableRef[A]): int
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t.

See also:

  • pairs iterator
  • keys iterator
  • mvalues iterator

entries.name: mvalues
entries.code: iterator mvalues[A](t: CountTableRef[A]): var int
entries.description:
Iterates over any value in the table t. The values can be modified.

See also:

  • mpairs iterator
  • values iterator

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[K, V](s: Table[K, V]): Hash

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[K, V](s: OrderedTable[K, V]): Hash

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[V](s: CountTable[V]): Hash
